key_insight: A ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 D-gun coating substantially improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V by promoting a protective tribo-layer, and machine learning can predict wear from test conditions.
engineering_relevance: This shows how detonation-sprayed ceramic coatings can extend the service life of titanium alloys in aerospace, biomedical, and marine tribological components.
method: Applied Al2O3-TiO2 coating to Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spraying under controlled parameters.
Evaluated wear with pin-on-disc testing per ASTM G99 across 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Built ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding conditions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that random forest had the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error, and SEM showed reduced wear with tribo-layer formation on coated surfaces.
watch_item: The model appears trained on a limited experimental window, so predictions may not generalize well outside the tested load, speed, and duration ranges.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
